turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing some pretty bad abdominal pain and bloody stools for the past three weeks, and I'm worried something might be seriously wrong with my gut. It's been really uncomfortable and it's affecting my work performance, so I need to get to the bottom of it.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: The pain is mostly on the left side of my abdomen, but it's crampy and can move around a bit. Sometimes it feels like a sharp stabbing sensation, other times it's just a dull ache that won't go away. And with the bloody stools, I'm getting really concerned - it's not just a little blood, there's usually a decent amount mixed in with my stool.

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: To be honest, yeah... sometimes after I've had one of these episodes, I feel really weak and fatigued. It's like I've been drained or something. And my joints ache even more than usual - I get pain in both knees and ankles, but it's not like there's swelling or anything, just this dull ache that's constant.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: No, no sweating or clamminess for me. But to be honest, I have been feeling pretty stressed lately because of work and these symptoms, so maybe that's contributed to my fatigue. And like I mentioned earlier, I've also noticed that I haven't had much of an appetite since this started, but I'm not sure if that's related or just a side effect of being tired all the time.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: No, there's been no sweating or clamminess for me. Like I said, it's mostly just this dull ache in my left abdomen and these bloody stools that are really freaking me out. And to be honest, I'm getting a little worried about what might be causing all of this...

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: The pain is pretty much always on the left side, but sometimes it'll radiate to my lower back and even my hips. It's hard to describe, but it feels like a wave of pain that moves around, you know? One minute it's in one spot, the next it's somewhere else, and it can be really unpredictable.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: No, the pain doesn't usually radiate to my arms, neck, or jaw. It tends to stay in my abdomen and lower back, with occasional waves of discomfort that move around within those areas. But like I said, it's pretty unpredictable, so sometimes it can feel like it's spreading to other parts of my body, but when I focus on it, the pain is usually still centered in my left abdomen or lower back.

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: No, there's no tingling or numbness in my arms. Like I said, the pain is mostly confined to my abdominal area and lower back, with some occasional waves of discomfort that move around within those areas. It's a really uncomfortable feeling, but I'm trying to stay focused on what's happening so we can figure out what's going on.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: No, nothing like that in my extremities. Like I said, it's mostly just this abdominal discomfort and bloody stools that are worrying me. Can you tell me what might be causing all of this? I've been feeling really anxious about what could be wrong with my gut.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: No, no swelling at all. In fact, I've lost a bit of weight over the past three weeks, which is probably not helping things. It's just been really hard to eat when I'm feeling so crampy and uncomfortable after meals.

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: No, my shoes still fit fine and there's no puffiness in my legs. But like I said, I have lost some weight unintentionally over the past three weeks, which is really concerning me because it feels like a sign that something's seriously wrong with my body. Can you please help me figure out what's going on?

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: To be honest, yeah... sometimes after I've had one of these bloody stool episodes, I'll feel a bit nauseous and my stomach will feel really upset. It's like my whole gut is just rebelling against me or something. But it's not always like that - sometimes I can eat a meal without any issues at all, but other times... well, you've heard the story by now, right?

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: Yeah, definitely. When the pain and bloody stools hit, my stomach feels like it's in knots and I get this queasy feeling that makes me want to just lie down and try to ride it out. It's not always nausea, but there's definitely a sense of discomfort and upset in my stomach that goes along with the pain. And like I said, sometimes after an episode, I'll feel even more queasy and nauseous, which is really not fun at all.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: No, no fevers, chills, or night sweats for me. Like I mentioned earlier, sometimes I do get a low-grade fever, but it's just a slight temperature elevation that comes and goes, usually only when I'm feeling particularly unwell. But nothing like a full-blown fever or any kind of sweating at night.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: I've already mentioned that I've been losing weight unintentionally over the past three weeks, and to be honest, my appetite has been pretty suppressed since this all started. I'm not really hungry most of the time, which is making it hard for me to eat regularly and keep my strength up.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient: My diet's been pretty irregular lately due to long work hours and stress. I've been eating a lot of takeout and fast food because it's convenient, but I'm not really hungry most of the time anyway. I used to be pretty active, but since this all started, I haven't had much energy for exercise or even just going for a walk. My daily routine is basically just work, come home, eat something if I can stomach it, and then try to rest because I'm so tired all the time. It's not exactly ideal, but I'm hoping that once we figure out what's going on with my gut, things will start to get back to normal.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: I used to be pretty active, like I said. I'd go for walks or jogs during my lunch break and even try to fit in a few gym sessions on weekends. But since this all started, it's been really hard to find the energy for that kind of thing. Now, most days I'm just lucky if I can make it from my car to the front door without feeling exhausted.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet's been pretty irregular lately. I've been eating a lot of takeout and fast food because it's convenient, but I'm not really hungry most of the time anyway. When I do eat, it's usually something quick like a burger or a sandwich. I used to try to make an effort to cook healthy meals at home, but since this all started, that's fallen by the wayside. Sometimes I'll have a bowl of cereal or toast if I'm feeling really down, but it's not exactly a balanced diet, and I know that's not helping things.

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: My mom is healthy as far as I know, no major issues with her. But my dad has type 2 diabetes, so that's something to consider, I suppose. And my older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was around 30 years old... that's been a concern for me lately, especially since I'm experiencing similar symptoms.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient: Yes, my dad has type 2 diabetes, and my older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was around 30 years old. To be honest, that's been on my mind a lot lately since I've been experiencing these symptoms... it's scary to think that there might be something like that going on in my own body.
